title: Cheelee Alternative: DeHub vs Cheelee vs Theta.tv vs XCAD (2026)
html:
If you are hunting for a Cheelee alternative, the short answer is that the four best-known watch-to-earn names in 2026 solve very different problems. Cheelee gates faster earnings behind paid NFT "glasses"; Theta.tv has largely folded its viewer-facing stream into an AI-and-infrastructure play; XCAD Network layers rewards on top of YouTube rather than running its own social feed; and DeHub, the decentralised social network, ties watch-to-earn to a full open-source platform you can join for free. This guide compares all four honestly on the things that actually decide whether you earn: how much it costs to start, whether payouts are verifiable, and what you get beyond the reward counter.

We have kept competitor descriptions general and current to 2026, because these products change quickly. Always check each platform's own documentation before you commit time or money. If you are new to the category, start with our explainer on what watch-to-earn is, then come back here to compare the options.

Why people look for a Cheelee alternative

Most searches for a Cheelee alternative come down to three frustrations. The first is cost: the biggest earnings on glasses-based apps are locked behind paid items, so "free to start" and "free to earn meaningfully" are not the same thing. The second is trust: withdrawal friction, fees and account issues generate a steady stream of "is it a scam" queries, and it is fair to want payouts you can independently verify. The third is depth: a reward counter on its own gets dull, and creators in particular want somewhere they actually own an audience.

The comparison below scores each platform against those three questions, then explains where DeHub fits. For the wider market, our roundup of the best watch-to-earn platforms of 2026 covers more names in less depth.

DeHub vs Cheelee: the NFT-glasses paywall

Cheelee is a short-video app where scrolling a feed earns in-app tokens, and it is the platform most people benchmark against. Everyone starts with a free pair of NFT "glasses", so you can technically earn without spending. The catch is that meaningful earning speed is tied to buying higher-rarity glasses, which have been reported to range from single digits up to a couple of hundred dollars, and those items carry durability and rarity mechanics that shape how much you actually take home.

That paywall is the core of the dehub vs cheelee decision. Independent reviews in 2026 are split: some describe a functioning app with real documentation and a dual-token model, while others flag withdrawal complications, conversion and commission fees often cited around 30%, plus gas costs, and a genuine risk of spending more on glasses than you earn back. None of that makes Cheelee an outright scam, but it does make it a speculative purchase rather than a free side income. If your main reason for wanting a Cheelee alternative is "I do not want to buy anything to begin", that is exactly the gap DeHub is built to fill. For a level-headed look at the wider question, see our piece on whether watch-to-earn is legit.

Theta.tv alternative: reward caps and an AI pivot

Theta built its name on decentralised video delivery, where viewers earned TFUEL partly by relaying bandwidth through edge nodes. It was one of the earliest credible watch-to-earn stories, and the underlying network is real infrastructure rather than a gimmick.

The important 2026 context for anyone seeking a theta.tv alternative is that the project has clearly repositioned. Since the 2024 launch of Theta EdgeCloud, the roadmap has pivoted hard from consumer video streaming towards decentralised GPU compute and AI inference, with edge nodes now rewarded for running compute jobs, not only for relaying video. That is a sensible business move, but it means the casual "sit and watch to earn" viewer experience is no longer the headline. If you specifically want a platform where watching content is the product rather than a legacy feature, Theta's shift is worth weighing carefully before you invest your time.

XCAD Network alternative: rewards bolted onto YouTube

XCAD Network took a clever route: rather than building a rival social feed, it rewards you for watching existing YouTube creators via a browser extension or a mobile app that runs YouTube in-app. Watch enough of an eligible video from a participating creator and you earn that creator's token, which can be converted to XCAD, with staking multipliers to boost the rate. It has attracted a serious roster of large creators, which is a real strength.

The consideration for an xcad network alternative is structural. Because XCAD sits on top of YouTube, your content, your audience and your reach still live inside a centralised platform that XCAD does not control, and rewards depend on which creators have opted in. It is a reward layer, not a home. Over time the project has broadened well beyond pure watch-to-earn into creator tokens, an ecosystem and adjacent products, so if you came for a simple "watch and earn" loop, expect more moving parts than the pitch suggests. If you would rather own your presence on the network itself, that is the difference DeHub is designed around.

How DeHub compares as a Cheelee alternative

DeHub, the decentralised social network approaches watch-to-earn from the opposite end. Instead of a reward mechanic in search of a platform, it is a full open-source, user-owned social network in which earning is one native feature among many. Here is how it answers the three questions that send people looking for an alternative in the first place.

No purchase to start earning

You do not buy glasses, a headset or any entry NFT to begin. You can join the DeHub app, watch content and take part in watch-to-earn bounties without an upfront spend. That removes the single biggest risk in the Cheelee model, where your earnings depend on how much you were willing to pay for better items. The DHB token ($DHB) is the ecosystem token if you choose to engage with it, but it is not a gate you must pay through to start.

On-chain-verifiable payouts

Because DeHub is built on-chain, rewards and the actions behind them are recorded on a public ledger you can inspect, rather than sitting in an opaque in-app balance you have to trust. Content is minted on-chain the moment you upload, the feed is chronological and transparent with no hidden ranking algorithm, and the primary chain is Base, an Ethereum Layer 2, with contracts also on BNB Chain and Polygon. $DHB is tradable on decentralised exchanges and on Coinbase, and is listed on CoinGecko and CoinMarketCap. Verifiability does not guarantee you will earn a fortune, and nobody honest can promise that, but it does mean the payout logic is auditable rather than a black box.

Watch-to-earn inside a full social network

This is the biggest practical difference. On DeHub, watch-to-earn is one thread in a much larger fabric:

  • Tokenised uploads you can publish free, as pay-per-view, or token-gated, so creators actually monetise their work.
  • On-chain tradable subscriptions with resale royalties, meaning a fan subscription is an asset the holder can resell and the creator still earns on. Our glossary explains tokenised subscriptions in full.
  • Live streaming on Livepeer that has scaled to 50,000+ concurrent viewers, with on-chain live streams and animated tips.
  • A play-to-earn arcade plus the flagship game Last Chad Standing, an MMA-style battle royale with licensed fighters.
  • Open-source end-to-end encrypted messaging, up to 90% ad-revenue share to the ecosystem, the DeHub Card off-ramp, DePIN infrastructure and a custom CDN with sub-200ms global latency.

In other words, where Cheelee gives you a feed to scroll and XCAD gives you a reward layer over someone else's platform, DeHub gives you somewhere to build an audience, publish, stream, message and earn, with watching as one entry point rather than the whole product.

The company behind it

DeHub is built by DeLabs Ltd, a United Kingdom company, with the project founded in 2021 and the UK entity incorporated in 2022. Its co-founders, Mike Hales and Indi Jay Cammish, also founded First Class Agency, the UK's number-one official TikTok LIVE partner agency, and DeHub sponsors the North Wales Crusaders rugby league club. That is a concrete, checkable corporate footprint, which matters when the category is full of anonymous teams. Do not confuse this DeHub with DePaul University's "DeHUB" student portal or Rowan University's "DEHub" digital engineering hub; they are unrelated projects that happen to share a name.

Which is the best watch to earn app for you?

There is no single winner, so match the tool to your goal:

  • Choose Cheelee if you are comfortable treating paid NFT glasses as a speculative purchase and you mainly want a short-video scrolling loop.
  • Choose Theta if you care more about decentralised infrastructure and AI compute than about a viewer-first watch-to-earn experience, given its 2026 pivot.
  • Choose XCAD Network if your viewing already lives on YouTube and you simply want a reward layer on top of creators who have opted in.
  • Choose DeHub if you want to start free, verify payouts on-chain, and earn inside a full social network with creator tools, streaming, games and tradable subscriptions rather than a single mechanic.

If the goal is the best watch to earn app to actually live on rather than dabble with, DeHub's breadth and zero-cost entry make it the strongest all-round Cheelee alternative in 2026. Ready to test it yourself? Download it on Google Play or open the DeHub app in your browser, and judge the payouts on-chain rather than on our word.

Frequently asked questions

Is there a free Cheelee alternative?

Yes. DeHub lets you join, watch and take part in watch-to-earn bounties without buying any entry item, whereas Cheelee ties faster earnings to paid NFT glasses. That zero-cost start is the main reason people choose DeHub as a Cheelee alternative. See is watch-to-earn legit for a realistic view of what free earning looks like.

Is DeHub better than Cheelee?

They are built differently. Cheelee is a short-video feed with a paid-glasses earning boost, while DeHub is a full decentralised social network where watch-to-earn sits alongside tokenised uploads, live streaming, games and tradable subscriptions. If you want no upfront cost and on-chain-verifiable payouts, DeHub is the stronger fit; if you only want a scroll-to-earn feed, Cheelee is closer to that narrow use case.

What happened to Theta.tv watch-to-earn?

Theta's underlying network remains active, but since launching Theta EdgeCloud in 2024 the project has pivoted heavily towards decentralised GPU compute and AI rather than a viewer-first streaming experience. Anyone wanting a pure watch-to-earn product should weigh that shift, which is why many people now look for a Theta.tv alternative.

How is XCAD Network different from DeHub?

XCAD rewards you for watching participating creators on YouTube through an extension or app, so your content and audience still live on a centralised platform. DeHub is its own decentralised network where you own your presence, publish on-chain and earn natively, making it a fuller XCAD Network alternative rather than a reward layer over someone else's site.

Can you really earn by watching videos in 2026?

You can earn small, ongoing rewards, but treat any promise of large passive income with caution. Earnings depend on the platform's model, fees, token value and how much you engage. Platforms with on-chain-verifiable payouts and no purchase requirement, such as DeHub, let you check the mechanics yourself rather than trusting an in-app balance. Compare the field in our best watch-to-earn platforms of 2026 guide.
